en: Hi, y'all. My name is Holly Cooper, owner of Fried Green Tomatoes from Nashville, Tennessee.
ko: 안녕하세요. 저는 내슈빌 출신 홀리 쿠퍼, 프라이드 그린 토마토 사장이에요.

en: And I'm seeking $200,000 in exchange for 15% equity of my company, Fried Green Tomatoes.
ko: 회사 지분 15%에 20만 달러를 투자받고 싶습니다.

en: Our food truck makes every item fresh to order. And our bacon, lettuce, fried green tomato sandwich is the favorite.
ko: 저희 푸드트럭은 모든 메뉴를 주문 즉시 만들어요. 베이컨, 상추, 프라이드 그린 토마토 샌드위치가 인기예요.

en: Other favorites are our grouper sandwich, a shrimp po' boy, we have a catfish sandwich.
ko: 그루퍼 샌드위치, 새우 포보이, 메기 샌드위치도 있어요.

en: But we're known best by visitors from all over the country for our hand-battered fried green tomatoes, fried pickles, and our secret sauce.
ko: 전국에서 온 손님들이 수제 프라이드 그린 토마토, 피클 튀김, 비밀 소스로 유명해요.

en: Our products are flying off the shelves in retail stores. So, Sharks, this business is ripe with opportunities.
ko: 소매점에서 제품이 불티나게 팔려요. 이 사업은 기회로 가득합니다.

en: So who'd like to help spread a little bit of Nashville's southern hospitality all over the country?
ko: 내슈빌의 남부 환대를 전국에 퍼뜨릴 분?

en: Are y'all hungry? We are. Let's go. Nice and easy. Great job. Good job, Holly.
ko: 배고프세요? 배고파요. 가자. 잘했어. 잘했어, 홀리.

en: Thank you. Now feed us. Okay.
ko: 고마워요. 이제 먹여줘. 네.

en: All right. Thank you. This is from our food truck. Beautiful. Thank you, Holly. Thank you.
ko: 자, 감사합니다. 푸드트럭에서 만든 거예요. 멋져요. 고마워, 홀리.

en: Here you are, babe. Thank you, Holly. Thank you. I'll get my mouth around this.
ko: 여기요. 고마워요. 한입 먹어볼게요.

en: Thank you. You want some? Thank you. All right. Oh.
ko: 고마워요. 드실래요? 감사합니다.

en: That's very good, Holly. This is great. Thank you. This is the best part of the job and the worst part of the job.
ko: 정말 맛있어, 홀리. 대단해. 이 일의 최고이자 최악인 부분이야.

en: Yeah, this is great. So we do a seasonal business of special events.
ko: 응, 대단해. 계절 특별 이벤트 사업을 해요.

en: However, I just purchased a truck that we're going to be taking out for more lunch events.
ko: 트럭을 새로 사서 점심 이벤트에 나갈 거예요.

en: Our food truck is actually a 16-foot trailer. Most of our events, we're doing $600 to $800 an hour.
ko: 16피트 트레일러예요. 대부분 이벤트에서 시간당 600~800달러를 벌어요.

en: Wow. Really? And we have customers waiting more than an hour to get their favorites.
ko: 정말? 손님들이 한 시간 이상 기다려요.

en: Wow. Holly, it's fantastic. I love it. The fried green tomato thing I just inhaled.
ko: 와. 홀리, 대단해. 프라이드 그린 토마토 그냥 흡입했어.

en: But there's so many food trucks. I mean, what's the deal? Well, actually, it's more than a food truck.
ko: 근데 푸드트럭이 너무 많잖아. 뭐가 다른 거야? 사실 푸드트럭 이상이에요.

en: We have a product that we sell to retailers nationwide. Which is this batter mix?
ko: 전국 소매점에 파는 제품이 있어요. 이 반죽 믹스?

en: Right. We took that product to market in 2016. We sold in 42 states about 600 stores.
ko: 맞아요. 2016년에 출시해서 42개 주 약 600개 매장에서 팔았어요.

en: 600? More boutique stores, and we'd like to get into more of the big box stores.
ko: 600개? 부티크 매장 위주고, 대형 매장에도 진출하고 싶어요.

en: The batters cost me $1.28. They retail for $8.99. What are your sales?
ko: 반죽 원가는 1.28달러. 소매가는 8.99달러. 매출은?

en: We did $268,000 last year. Good for you. So it's all of your sales collectively.
ko: 작년에 26만 8천 달러. 대단해. 전부 합산이에요.

en: Right. So, Holly, tell us about you. I was in the restaurant business for about 18 years.
ko: 맞아요. 홀리, 자기소개 해줘. 요식업에서 18년 일했어요.

en: I actually owned a small cafe and actually sold that business. And then I got into commercial sales.
ko: 작은 카페를 운영하다 팔고 상업 영업을 했어요.

en: For what kind of commercial sales? Commercial fuel. I've also sold plumbing, HVAC business, electrical business.
ko: 어떤 상업 영업? 상업용 연료. 배관, 냉난방, 전기도요.

en: Awesome. Wow. So you can sell anything. You can sell anything, honey. I can sell it. Why did you leave sales to go back into the food business?
ko: 대단해. 뭐든 팔 수 있겠네. 뭐든 팔 수 있어요. 왜 영업을 그만두고 음식으로 돌아왔어요?

en: Oh, actually, I lost my job. Oh. I lost my job. I was the number one sales rep for the company.
ko: 사실 해고됐어요. 회사 1위 영업 사원이었는데, 전국 3위였어요. 주 계약을 따냈는데 해고당했어요.

en: I was number three in the nation. And I had just sold a state contract, and they let me go.
ko: 그래서 사업을 시작했어요. 제 운명을 내가 결정하려고요.

en: So that's why I started my business, so I could be in control of my destiny.
ko: 잘했어. 해고된 이유를 알려줄게. 네 재능을 질투한 거야. 저도 그렇게 생각해요.

en: Good for you. Well, I'm going to tell you why they fired you. They were jealous of your talent. I think so.
ko: 언제든 자기 운명을 자기 손에 쥘 수 있어. 그게 자유이고 성공이야.

en: But the beauty is is you can, at any point in time, take your life and your destiny into your own hands, and you can create a business or a product, and that's freedom and that's success.
ko: 네. 목표가 뭐예요? 푸드트럭을 프랜차이즈하고 싶어요.

en: Yes. What is your aspiration? I would love to be able to franchise our food truck.
ko: 프랜차이즈. 12명 넘는 사람이 사고 싶다고 했는데 방법을 몰라서 도움이 필요해요.

en: You want to franchise. And I have had more than a dozen people want to buy a franchise, and I just don't really know how to do that, so I'd love some help.
ko: 부정적이려는 건 아닌데 솔직해야 해. 20만 달러에 15%면 130만 달러 가치인데.

en: Not to be negative, but, you know, I have to speak the truth. I am Mr. Wonderful. $200,000 for 15% means your value of the business for $1.3 million.
ko: 130만 달러를 지불하기엔 어려워.

en: To pay $1.3 million for this business is very difficult.
ko: 이미 수익을 내는 프랜차이즈가 있으면 성장시킬 수 있다는 게 믿어지지만.

en: If you had an already established series of franchisees that were making money, that makes it more believable that you can pour gasoline on the fire and make it bigger.
ko: 아직 없잖아. 음식은 환상적인데 너무 작아.

en: But you don't have that yet. Look, I think the food's fantastic, but it's just too small.
ko: 투자 가치가 없어. 나는 빠질게. 이해해요.

en: It's not investable. I'm out. I understand.
ko: 레스토랑도 했었는데 푸드트럭이 카페보다 더 벌었어요.

en: Like I said, I've had restaurants, and I've done more dollars out of a food truck than I've done in a cafe.
ko: 정말 대단한 일을 했어. 자영업이 더 재미있을 거야.

en: What you've done is really quite amazing, and I'm sure you're having a whole lot more fun working for yourself.
ko: 맞아요. 먹은 거 좋았지만 건강식 위주라서 제품이 안 맞아요.

en: That's exactly right. I loved what I ate, but I'm that person that goes for the healthy, the light, the low-fat, so the product's not right for me.
ko: 죄송해요. 빠질게. 감사합니다.

en: I'm sorry. I'm out. Thank you. Thank you.
ko: 홀리, 26만 8천 달러 매출이 트럭과 제품 합산이죠.

en: Holly, you have $268,000 in sales, and that is a combination of the truck as well as the product and retailers.
ko: 네. 딸을 정규직으로 데려왔어요. 둘이면 올해 2배 이상 가능해요.

en: Yes. I actually just brought my daughter on full-time. So with the two of us, we can definitely more than double those numbers this year.
ko: 딸을 데려온 건 좋지만 투자를 받으려면 직원 5명, 10명으로 늘려야 해요.

en: It's nice that you brought in your daughter, but in order to really take an investment and accelerate the growth, you have to get to that point where, okay, how can I get to five employees?
ko: 아직 투자 가치가 없어요. 빠질게.

en: How can I get to ten employees? And that's something really that an entrepreneur has to go through. I just don't think you're investable yet, so for those reasons, I'm out.
ko: 축하해요. 커즌스 메인 랍스터 형제가 왔을 때 가장 중요한 질문은 하루 매출이었어요.

en: But congratulations. I remember when the Cousins Maine Lobster boys came in here, the two cousins, the most important question they answered was, how much are you doing a day?
ko: 3개월밖에 안 됐는데 하루 5천 달러라고 했어요. 저희는 하루 6400달러가 최고였어요.

en: And they had only been in business three months. Yes. And they said $5,000 a day. We've done $6,400 a day.
ko: 일주일 내내 열면 연 3백만 달러 이상이네. 갑자기 바브가 계산을 잘하네.

en: It's been our top. So that's more than $3 million a year if you had your trucks open six or seven days a week.
ko: 이런 계산은 할 수 있지. 넌 영업 천재잖아.

en: All of a sudden, Barb can do math. Well, this kind of math I can do because I know it inside and out. You obviously can sell like crazy.
ko: 맞아요. 나는 너의 꿈의 파트너야. 근데 대가를 잘 받아야 해. 2년 안에 부자로 만들어줄 수 있어.

en: I can. Well, I am your dream partner. Okay, but I'm going to have to be very well paid because I can do so much for you, and I can make you rich within two years, no doubt in my mind.
ko: 세상에. 이것으로 프랜차이즈 제국을 만들 수 있어. 형제들이 가진 중요한 재료는 영업력과 조직력이야.

en: Oh, my Lord. Let me tell you something. You can make an empire of franchises out of this, but the important ingredients that those two cousins have is, boy, can they sell, and boy, are they organized.
ko: 그 결과 3년 만에 6천만 달러 매출이야. 알아요. 그들처럼 되고 싶어요. 될 거야.

en: As a result of that, they have $60 million in sales three years later. I know. I want to be them. Yeah. You will be them.
ko: 20만 달러에 30% 제안할게.

en: No doubt in my mind you will be them. So I'll offer you the $200,000 for 30% of your business.
ko: 당연하지. 아냐, 생각해봐. 잠깐만.

en: That's a no-brainer. No, think about it. Wait a minute.
ko: 바바라가 달려드는 걸 보고, 20만 달러에 25% 제안할게.

en: Now that I saw Barbara all over this like a truffle pig on a truffle, I'll give you $200,000 for 25%.
ko: 프랜차이즈 사업을 모르잖아. 그래서? 여기 있어. 남자잖아.

en: And he doesn't know a damn thing about the franchise business. So? Stay here. Stay here. And he's a guy.
ko: 사랑해요. 진짜로. 뭐 할 거야? 데이먼 할인으로 5% 절약했네.

en: I love you. I really do. What are you going to do? You saved 5% on the Damon discount.
ko: 네. 바바라, 25%로 할 수 있어요? 경험이 있잖아요.

en: Yes. Barbara, could we get to 25? I know you've got the experience.
ko: 그 가치가 있어. 가치가 있어. 나는 이 분야 천재야. 정말 다들 사랑해요.

en: She's worth it. I'm worth it. I'm a genius at this. I really love you guys.
ko: 다 사랑해요. 바브 제안을 받을게요.

en: I love all of you. I think I'm going to take Barb's.
ko: 축하해. 미안해. 더 비싼 걸 택했네.

en: Congratulations. I'm sorry. She paid more. I'm sorry.
ko: 좋아. 미안해. 저 남자 떼어냈어. 그러니까. 영리해. 고마워.

en: I love it. I'm sorry. We got rid of that guy. I know. I know. You're so smart. Thank you, doll.
ko: 감사합니다. 안됐네, 데이먼. 튀김 사업에선 마른 사람을 믿으면 안 돼.

en: Thank you. Too bad, Damon. Too bad, Damon. In the fried food business, never trust the skinny version.
ko: 알아. 나를 택했어야지. 축하해. 딜이 될 줄 몰랐는데.

en: I know. I know. I'm your guy. Congratulations. I would have never thought you'd get a deal.
ko: 감사합니다. 됐네. 축하해, 홀리. 아기한테 사탕 뺏기였어. 왜 그 사업 안 원했어?

en: Thank you. And you did. Congratulations, Holly. It was like taking candy from a baby. Why didn't you guys want that business?
ko: 금광인데. 샤크탱크에선 무슨 일이 벌어질지 모르지.

en: It's a gold mine. You never know what's going to happen on Shark Tank.
ko: 바바라를 선택한 건 더 많은 지분을 줄 가치가 있었어요. 경험이 필요했고, 바바라가 원하던 샤크였으니까요.

en: It was worth giving up a little more equity to get Barbara because I knew that she had the experience to do what we needed, and she's the shark I was after, and so we're thrilled to have her as a partner.
